_comment: data/horologion/typika-lectionary.json
Byzantine Sunday Liturgy lectionary — Typika epistle and gospel.
Key: Sunday After Pentecost integer string '1'–'31'.
Tone and Sunday After Pentecost are independent cycles. Do not index by tone.
Tradition family: Slavic / OCA / ROCOR / Antiochian.
Source: ByzCath.org Byzantine Lectionary, Great Church / Constantinople tradition.
Weeks 22–30 use the Slavic gospel sequence. Greek variants are metadata only.
Week 1 All Saints is type:special and remains rubric fallback until composite rendering is explicitly enabled.
Week 19 epistle is cross-chapter and stored as segments.

1.label: First Sunday After Pentecost (All Saints)
1.note: All Saints Sunday. Gospel is composite. Engine emits rubric fallback for type:special.

19.label: Nineteenth Sunday After Pentecost
19.note: Cross-chapter epistle. Stored as segments; plain epistle field absent.

holy-fathers.label: Sunday of the Holy Fathers of the First Council

pentecost.label: Pentecost Sunday
pentecost.note: Gospel is composite; stored as segments.

pre_lenten._comment: Pre-Lenten Triodion Sundays. Keyed by liturgical name. days_before_pascha counts from upcoming Pascha. Tradition family: Slavic/OCA/ROCOR/Antiochian.

zacchaeus.label: Sunday of Zacchaeus
zacchaeus.tradition_note: Greek/Antiochian tradition only; not routed until a tradition-variant gate exists.

publican-pharisee.label: Sunday of the Publican and Pharisee

palm_sunday.label: Palm Sunday — Entrance of the Lord into Jerusalem

bright_week._comment: Bright Week Typika readings. Keyed by weekday after Pascha; pascha_offset counts days after Pascha.

_comment: Ordinary Byzantine weekday Typika Epistle/Gospel readings.
Keyed by ordinary_week_after_pentecost + weekday.
Week 1 begins Monday of the Holy Spirit, the day after Pentecost.
Saturday is a keyed weekday under the same week but uses its own Saturday row from the lectionary.

Scope of this data family: Weeks 1-17, Monday-Saturday. Gospel rows serve the ordinary pre-Lukan cycle; after the Lukan boundary, Epistle rows may continue as ordinary-cycle data while Gospel resolves from lukan_weekday_gospels.
Current populated tranche: Weeks 1-33, Monday-Saturday. Weeks 18-33 are Epistle support rows for the post-Lukan split-key cycle.
Lukan Jump / Luke-cycle Gospel readings are modeled separately in lukan_weekday_gospels.
Menaion and fixed-feast overlays are out of scope; this table is ordinary-cycle fallback data.

_comment: Weekday Gospel cycle after the Lukan Jump.
Begins Monday following the Sunday after the Exaltation of the Cross.
Gospel-only table; Epistles continue from ordinary_weekdays_after_pentecost where ordinary-cycle Epistle data exists.
Current populated tranche: Weeks 1-12, Monday-Saturday.
Menaion and fixed-feast overlays are out of scope.

_comment: Post-Lukan weekday Gospel cycle after the true Monday-Saturday Lukan weekday table ends.
Keyed by ordinary_week_after_pentecost + weekday, not by lukan_week.
After Lukan Week 12 / ordinary Week 28; post-Lukan Mark/Luke rows begin at ordinary Week 29, Monday-Friday use Mark while Saturday retains Luke.
Current populated tranche: ordinary Weeks 29-33. Week 34+ belongs to the pre-Lenten/Triodion weekday family, not this ordinary post-Lukan family.
Nativity/Theophany and Triodion/pre-Lenten interruption gates are engine-level guards; fixed-feast overlays remain out of scope.

_comment: General Typika Epistle/Gospel overlay family for fixed and relative feast readings.
First populated family: winter_cycle for Nativity/Theophany core fixed days and relative Sundays.
This is Scripture citation metadata only; Royal Hours, hymns, prokeimena, alleluia verses, antiphons, and full Menaion propers remain out of scope.
If a fixed day and relative Sunday collide, readings are appended/combined rather than silently replacing one another.
Ordinary/Lukan/post-Lukan weekday readings should continue on afterfeast weekdays where no overlay key applies.
Core winter-cycle fixed and relative Sunday readings.
Relative Saturdays and full afterfeast/forefeast weekday overlays are deferred.
Nativity Eve and Theophany Eve use service_context metadata for Vesperal Liturgy readings; Royal Hours readings are explicitly out of scope.

nativity_saturday_before.label: Saturday before Nativity
nativity_saturday_before.date_rule: Saturday before Nativity, normally between December 18 and December 24 inclusive.

nativity_sunday_before.label: Sunday before Nativity / Holy Fathers
nativity_sunday_before.date_rule: Sunday between December 18 and December 24 inclusive.

12-24_nativity_eve.label: Eve of the Nativity of Christ

weekend_alternate.note: Saturday/Sunday collision practice may appoint Galatians 3:15-22 with Luke 2:1-20 at Vespers. Royal Hours and transferred-service handling remain out of scope for this tranche.

12-25_nativity.label: Nativity of Our Lord God and Savior Jesus Christ

nativity_saturday_after.label: Saturday after Nativity
nativity_saturday_after.date_rule: Saturday after Nativity, normally between December 26 and January 1 depending on the weekday of Nativity.

nativity_sunday_after.label: Sunday after Nativity
nativity_sunday_after.date_rule: Sunday between December 26 and December 31 inclusive; also appended on December 26 when Nativity itself falls on Sunday.

01-01_circumcision_basil.label: Circumcision of Christ and St Basil the Great

theophany_saturday_before.label: Saturday before Theophany
theophany_saturday_before.date_rule: Saturday before Theophany, between January 1 and January 5 inclusive.

theophany_sunday_before.label: Sunday before Theophany
theophany_sunday_before.date_rule: Sunday between January 1 and January 5 inclusive.

01-07_synaxis_forerunner.label: Synaxis of the Holy Glorious Prophet, Forerunner, and Baptist John

theophany_saturday_after.label: Saturday after Theophany
theophany_saturday_after.date_rule: Saturday after Theophany, between January 7 and January 13 inclusive.

theophany_sunday_after.label: Sunday after Theophany
theophany_sunday_after.date_rule: Sunday between January 7 and January 13 inclusive.

_comment: Major fixed-date feast overlays beyond the winter cycle.
Annunciation is intentionally excluded from this tranche pending Lent/Pascha collision policy.
These entries are Scripture citation metadata only; feast propers, hymns, prokeimena, alleluia verses, and full annual Typikon suppression rules remain out of scope.
Fixed-date overlays append/combine with temporal readings under the current policy.

02-02_meeting_lord.label: Meeting of our Lord God and Savior Jesus Christ in the Temple

03-25_annunciation.label: Annunciation of our Most Holy Lady, Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary
03-25_annunciation.collision_policy: guarded_fixed_overlay; Sunday and Lazarus-or-later collisions deferred pending annual Typikon merge policy
03-25_annunciation.note: Annunciation is allowed as a guarded fixed-date overlay on non-Sunday March 25 dates before Lazarus Saturday. Sunday and Lazarus-or-later collisions require a later temporal-feast merge / annual Typikon policy.

05-08_john_theologian.label: Holy Apostle and Evangelist John the Theologian
05-08_john_theologian.note: Discontinuous Gospel stored as segments for resolver compatibility.

06-24_nativity_forerunner.label: Nativity of the Holy Glorious Prophet, Forerunner and Baptist John

06-29_peter_paul.label: Holy Glorious and All-Praised Leaders of the Apostles, Peter and Paul

06-30_synaxis_twelve_apostles.label: Synaxis of the Holy Glorious and All-Praised Twelve Apostles

08-06_transfiguration.label: Holy Transfiguration of our Lord God and Savior Jesus Christ

08-15_dormition_theotokos.label: Dormition of our Most Holy Lady, Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary

08-29_beheading_forerunner.label: Beheading of the Holy Glorious Prophet, Forerunner and Baptist John

09-08_nativity_theotokos.label: Nativity of our Most Holy Lady, Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary

09-14_exaltation_cross.label: Universal Exaltation of the Precious and Life-Giving Cross
09-14_exaltation_cross.note: Fixed-date Exaltation readings only; Sunday before/after Exaltation and Lukan Jump boundary logic remain separate.

10-26_demetrios.label: Holy, Glorious Demetrios the Myrrh-gusher of Thessaloniki

11-08_archangels.label: Synaxis of the Holy Bodiless Heavenly Powers

11-13_john_chrysostom.label: Our Father among the Saints John Chrysostom, Archbishop of Constantinople

11-21_entry_theotokos.label: Entry of our Most Holy Lady, Theotokos and Ever-Virgin Mary, into the Temple

11-30_apostle_andrew.label: Holy and All-Praised Apostle Andrew the First-Called

12-06_st_nicholas.label: Holy Hierarch Nicholas, Archbishop of Myra, Wonderworker

_comment: Temporal weekday Epistle/Gospel metadata for the pre-Lenten Triodion period.
Keys are named by liturgical week, not ordinary after-Pentecost lookup.
publican_pharisee = Week 34 after Pentecost, Monday-Saturday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday.
meatfare = Week 35 after Pentecost, Monday-Saturday after Prodigal Son Sunday, culminating in Meatfare Sunday.
cheesefare = Week 36 after Pentecost, Monday-Saturday after Meatfare Sunday, culminating in Forgiveness Sunday.

Cheesefare Wednesday and Friday have no Divine Liturgy Epistle/Gospel in the temporal table; Sixth Hour and Vespers Old Testament readings belong to office-reading handling, not these Typika Epistle/Gospel slots.
Fixed-feast and Menaion overlays remain out of scope.

publican_pharisee.label: Week after Publican and Pharisee Sunday

monday.label: Monday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday

tuesday.label: Tuesday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday

wednesday.label: Wednesday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday

thursday.label: Thursday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday
thursday.note: Gospel begins at Mark 13:31, overlapping the prior day's endpoint; source-governed.

friday.label: Friday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday

saturday.label: Saturday after Publican and Pharisee Sunday

monday.label: Monday of Meatfare Week
monday.note: Source ends at 1 John 3:10a; resolver uses full verse 10.

tuesday.label: Tuesday of Meatfare Week
tuesday.note: Source begins at 1 John 3:10b; resolver uses full verse 10.

wednesday.label: Wednesday of Meatfare Week

thursday.label: Thursday of Meatfare Week
thursday.note: Mark 15:1 overlaps the prior day's endpoint; source-governed.

friday.label: Friday of Meatfare Week
friday.note: Discontinuous Gospel.

saturday.label: Meatfare Saturday / Saturday of Souls
saturday.note: Special Saturday with day and departed reading sets.

monday.label: Monday of Cheesefare Week

tuesday.label: Tuesday of Cheesefare Week
tuesday.note: Source begins second Gospel segment at Luke 22:45b; resolver uses full verse 45.

wednesday.label: Wednesday of Cheesefare Week
wednesday.rubric: No Divine Liturgy Epistle/Gospel is appointed in the temporal table for Cheesefare Wednesday. Sixth Hour and Vespers Old Testament readings belong to office-reading handling and are not rendered in these Typika Epistle/Gospel slots.

thursday.label: Thursday of Cheesefare Week

friday.label: Friday of Cheesefare Week
friday.rubric: No Divine Liturgy Epistle/Gospel is appointed in the temporal table for Cheesefare Friday. Sixth Hour and Vespers Old Testament readings belong to office-reading handling and are not rendered in these Typika Epistle/Gospel slots.

saturday.label: Cheesefare Saturday / Commemoration of Holy Ascetics
saturday.note: Special Saturday with day and Holy Ascetics reading sets.

great_lent._comment: Great Lent Sundays. Keyed by liturgical name. days_before_pascha counts from upcoming Pascha. Tradition family: Slavic/OCA/ROCOR/Antiochian. Source: ByzCath.org Byzantine Lectionary.

mary-of-egypt.label: Sunday of St. Mary of Egypt
